feeds.title: bioRxiv Subject Collection: Bioinformatics
feeds.tldr: bioRxiv Subject Collection: Bioinformatics currently has 11 retained item(s) on the page. Key items include "PANDA: Read-Level Phased Analysis of DNA Amplicons for Methylation Studies", "Proteome analyses reveal Endoplasmic Reticulum stress-induced changes in protein abundance associated with Ube2j2 deficiency in human cell culture", and "Importance of taking Single Amino Acid Variant and accessory proteome variability into account in Data Independent Acquisition Proteomics: illustrated with Legionella pneumophila analysis".
highlights: PANDA: Read-Level Phased Analysis of DNA Amplicons for Methylation Studies
Proteome analyses reveal Endoplasmic Reticulum stress-induced changes in protein abundance associated with Ube2j2 deficiency in human cell culture
Importance of taking Single Amino Acid Variant and accessory proteome variability into account in Data Independent Acquisition Proteomics: illustrated with Legionella pneumophila analysis

items.title: PANDA: Read-Level Phased Analysis of DNA Amplicons for Methylation Studies
items.tldr: DNA methylation analysis using bisulfite sequencing is widely used to investigate epigenetic regulation at single-base resolution; however, conventional analysis workflows primarily rely on site-wise averaging, which obscures contiguous methylation patterns e…

items.title: Proteome analyses reveal Endoplasmic Reticulum stress-induced changes in protein abundance associated with Ube2j2 deficiency in human cell culture
items.tldr: The unfolded protein response (UPR) helps reinstate cellular proteostasis upon an accumulation of misfolded proteins in the endoplasmic reticulum (ER), in part through ER-associated degradation (ERAD). Ube2j2 is an ER-localized E2 ubiquitin-conjugating enzyme…

items.title: Anonymized Somatic Tumor Twins (STTs) enable open genome data sharing and use in research and clinical oncology
items.tldr: The study of somatic variants from tumor genomes is fundamental to cancer research and clinical decision-making. However, existing data protection frameworks impose restrictions on the use and sharing of these variants in conjunction with sensitive germline i…

items.title: LigandForge: A Web Server for Structure-Guided De Novo Drug Design
items.tldr: Despite advances in computational drug discovery, de novo drug design remains hindered by high licensing costs and the need for specialized programming expertise. We present LigandForge, a webserver for structure-guided de novo ligand generation.

items.title: Conserved water molecules as structural ligands modulating pathogenic variation in human protein binding sites
items.tldr: Conserved water molecules (CWMs) are tightly bound solvent molecules that occupy well-defined and recurrent positions in protein structures. Although they are known to influence protein stability, function, and ligand binding, their contribution to human gene…

items.title: muat: portable transformer-based method for tumour classification and representation learning from somatic variants
items.tldr: Motivation: Deep neural networks have proven effective in classifying tumour types using next-generation sequencing data. However, developing transferable models that work across heterogeneous operating environments remains challenging due to differences in c…

items.title: EMITS: expectation-maximization abundance estimation for fungal ITS communities from long-read sequencing
items.tldr: EMITS: expectation-maximization abundance estimation for fungal ITS communities from long-read sequencing

items.tldr: TF-IDF k-mer-based Classical and Hybrid Machine Learning Models for SARS-CoV-2 Variant Classification under Imbalanced Genomic Data

items.tldr: Benchmarking Heritability Estimation Strategies Across 86 Configurations and Their Downstream Effect on Polygenic Risk Score Performance

feeds.title: Cell
feeds.tldr: This digest features a paper on cellular senescence in cancer. The paper defines senescence by six major hallmarks as a program that halts cell division while rewiring chromatin, metabolism, microenvironment sensing, and immune interactions. It describes the dual role of senescence in suppressing or promoting cancer and its potential as a frontier for precision therapy.
highlights: Defined by six major hallmarks
Halts cell division and rewires cellular processes
Dual role in cancer suppression and promotion
Exciting frontier for precision therapy

items.title: Senescence in cancer: Hallmarks, paradoxes, and therapeutic promise
items.tldr: The paper discusses cellular senescence in cancer, characterized by six hallmarks, which stops cell division and alters chromatin, metabolism, and immune interactions. It highlights the paradoxical roles in cancer and the therapeutic promise for precision medicine.

feeds.title: Nature
feeds.tldr: The Trump administration has proposed massive budget cuts for US science agencies in its 2027 spending plan, with NSF facing a 55% reduction and EPA and NASA science divisions over 50% cuts. The plan dissolves NSF's social sciences directorate, bans federal funds for some academic journal subscriptions, and cuts basic quantum and AI research while boosting applied funding at defense and energy departments. Congress will negotiate the final budget, having rejected similar cuts in 2026.
highlights: NSF budget cut 55% to $4 billion
EPA and NASA science divisions face >50% reductions
NSF social sciences directorate dissolved
Ban on federal journal subscription funding; applied quantum/AI research increased

items.title: Massive budget cuts for US science proposed again by Trump administration
items.tldr: The 2027 budget proposal from the Trump administration includes a 55% cut to NSF, elimination of its social sciences division, a ban on federal funding for some journal subscriptions, and cuts to basic quantum/AI research with increased applied funding at defense departments.

feeds.title: Nature Communications
feeds.tldr: This digest features 16 recent papers from Nature Communications spanning photonics, biotechnology, environmental science, and biomedicine. Key advances include techniques for sculpting topological photonic modes, a DNA storage codec that reduces redundancy, and insights into marine phage-bacteria dynamics. AI applications range from 3D molecule generation to healthcare data harmonization, while studies address glacier outburst floods, permafrost thaw, and neural mechanisms of addiction.
highlights: Sculpting topological photonic modes using artificial gauge fields and neural networks.
Gungnir codec enables reliable DNA data recovery from single error-prone sequences.
Oysters identified as hotspots for gene exchange in marine phage-bacteria systems.

items.title: Artificial gauge fields for sculpting topological modes on photonic chips
items.tldr: Authors propose combining artificial gauge fields with neural networks to design energy landscapes, transforming topological modes into arbitrary shapes on photonic chips.

items.title: Gungnir codec enabling high error-tolerance and low-redundancy DNA storage through substantial computing power
items.tldr: Gungnir is a DNA storage codec that recovers data from a single, highly error-prone sequence, reducing reliance on redundant copies and deep sequencing.

items.title: Complex temporal dynamics of phage-bacteria populations in an animal-associated marine system
items.tldr: Study of over 1000 phages and 600 bacterial hosts reveals oysters as hotspots for gene exchange in marine phage-bacteria dynamics.

items.title: Disentangled autoencoding equivariant diffusion model for controlled generation of 3D molecules
items.tldr: A semantics-guided diffusion model enables precise and data-efficient multi-property control for generating 3D molecules, accelerating drug discovery.

items.title: Subglacial water flow and ice dynamics during glacial lake outburst floods observed from space
items.tldr: Satellite data analysis tracks approximately 1 km³ of flood water draining under a glacier in 3 weeks, providing insights for predicting glacier and ice sheet behavior.

items.title: Social reward outcompetes drug seeking dopaminergic ensembles to prevent relapse
items.tldr: Social reward and drug relapse activate distinct dopaminergic populations in the VTA that compete to regulate behavioral outcomes, with therapeutic implications for addiction.

items.title: Representation learning to advance multi-institutional studies with electronic health record data from US and France
items.tldr: A framework harmonizes electronic health record data across hospitals using medical knowledge, large language models, and graph learning, enabling analysis without sharing patient-level data.

items.title: Hippo signaling differentially regulates distal progenitor subpopulations and their transitional states to construct the mammalian lungs
items.tldr: A subpopulation of distal SOX9+ lung progenitors controls lung size through bifurcation and transitions to generate conducting airway and alveolar cell types.

items.title: Permafrost landsystems define regional variability in climate change effects on northern environments
items.tldr: Permafrost landsystems, defined by landform assemblages and ecosystems, vary with geological and climatic legacies to predict regional consequences of thaw.

items.title: Mechanofusion-derived cathode composite microstructures with scalable mixed conducting matrix coatings for solid state batteries
items.tldr: Solvent-free mechanofusion creates mixed conducting matrix coatings on cathode particles, with coating quality linked to mixing stresses for scalable solid-state battery manufacturing.

items.title: A highly limited amino acid library from asteroid Bennu yields wide-ranging protein folds
items.tldr: AI-based protein design shows that small primordial amino acid libraries, constrained by asteroid Bennu data, can form a wide range of protein folds, informing origins of life.

items.title: Probing ultrafast heating and ionization dynamics in solid density plasmas with time-resolved resonant X-ray absorption and emission
items.tldr: Combining resonant X-ray emission spectroscopy and absorption imaging from an XFEL probe achieves sub-picosecond resolution of heating and ionization in solid-density plasmas.

items.title: Solution-processed photovoltaic and thermoelectric hybrid systems with efficiency exceeding 50%
items.tldr: Solution-processed photovoltaic-thermoelectric hybrid systems optimized for heat utilization and current matching demonstrate a maximum efficiency of 50.28%.

items.title: A conversational multi-agent AI system for automated plant phenotyping
items.tldr: An AI system integrating large language models with a phenotype toolkit automates crop analysis, reducing technical barriers and enhancing accessibility.

items.title: Distinct mechanisms of replication stress induced by oncogenic RAS and cyclin E1 converge on R-loop-dependent fork reversal
items.tldr: Oncogenic RAS induces replication stress through ROS-driven replisome alterations, leading to R-loop-dependent fork reversal, with distinct mechanisms converging from RAS and cyclin E1.

items.title: Influence of the broadly neutralizing antibody VRC01 on HIV breakthrough virus populations in antibody-mediated prevention trials
items.tldr: Deep sequencing of AMP trial participants shows 38% multilineage HIV infections, including coinfection with sensitive and resistant viruses, and demonstrates VRC01 does not alter the transmission bottleneck.

feeds.title: Nature Computational Science
feeds.tldr: This digest features two papers from Nature Computational Science. The first introduces HorusEye, a self-supervised foundation model that learns noise from X-ray scans for universal tomography restoration without clean training data. The second presents a benchmark for spatial alignment methods in spatial transcriptomics, showing that the best approach is dataset dependent and offering actionable guidance for future development.
highlights: HorusEye model enables robust tomography restoration across diverse modalities without clean data.
Benchmark finds spatial transcriptomics alignment methods are dataset dependent.
Both studies provide actionable insights for computational science method development.

items.title: Universal restoration of medical images
items.tldr: A self-supervised foundation model, HorusEye, learns realistic noise directly from X-ray scans to enable robust tomography restoration across diverse modalities, scanners, and tasks without requiring clean training data.

items.title: Benchmarking alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics data
items.tldr: A benchmark evaluating spatial alignment methods for spatial transcriptomics demonstrates that the best approach is dataset dependent and offers actionable guidance for future method development.

feeds.title: Nature Health
feeds.tldr: Nature Health's daily digest features a single paper on GLP-1 receptor agents in longevity research. The title advocates for rigorous testing of agonists as longevity therapeutics, but the provided text discusses antagonists as transitional and emphasizes the need for more evidence on their effects, highlighting a discrepancy in the source material.
highlights: Title: GLP-1 receptor agonists should be rigorously tested as longevity therapeutics
Text: GLP-1 receptor antagonists classified as transitional longevity therapeutics
Text: More evidence needed to assess direct and indirect effects

items.title: GLP-1 receptor agonists should be rigorously tested as longevity therapeutics
items.tldr: The paper's title calls for testing GLP-1 receptor agonists for longevity benefits, while the text snippet describes GLP-1 receptor antagonists as transitional and requiring additional research.

feeds.title: Nature Medicine
feeds.tldr: This Nature Medicine digest includes studies on monkeypox virus lineage surveillance, global exposome impacts on brain aging, and therapeutic benchmarks for Alzheimer's disease. The findings emphasize genomic monitoring for recombination risks, differential effects of social and physical exposures on brain structure and function, and the need for clear targets to accelerate Alzheimer's treatment development.
highlights: Monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 identified in Congo with co-circulation of clades Ia, Ib, and IIb, highlighting surveillance needs due to recombination risks.
Exposome analyses across 34 countries link social exposures to faster functional brain aging and physical exposures to faster structural brain aging.
Target product profiles outlined for Alzheimer's therapies to delay or prevent symptoms, aiming to speed development and set benchmarks.

items.title: Evidence of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of clade Ia, Ib and clade IIb
items.tldr: Whole-genome phylogenetic analyses identified a case of monkeypox virus clade IIb lineage A.2.2 in the Republic of the Congo and co-circulation of clades Ia, Ib, and IIb, emphasizing improved surveillance to mitigate future recombination events.

items.title: The exposome of brain aging across 34 countries
items.tldr: Exposome analyses across 34 countries showed that social exposures are associated with faster functional brain aging and physical exposures with faster structural brain aging.

items.title: Target product profiles for treatments to delay or prevent symptomatic Alzheimer’s disease
items.tldr: Authors outline target product profiles for therapies designed to delay or prevent the onset of clinical symptoms of Alzheimer's disease, providing benchmarks to accelerate therapeutic development.

feeds.title: Nature Methods
feeds.tldr: This feed features a paper introducing CREsted, a software toolkit for large-scale modeling of cis-regulatory logic in enhancers. CREsted leverages single-cell chromatin accessibility atlases to provide data-driven insights into regulatory elements driving cell type-specific gene regulation. The approach aims to decipher the genomic regulatory code across various tissues and species.
highlights: CREsted: toolkit for modeling enhancer cis-regulatory logic
Utilizes single-cell chromatin accessibility atlases
Applicable to multiple tissues and species

items.title: A toolkit for modeling cis -regulatory logic of enhancers at large scale
items.tldr: The paper presents CREsted, a software package that models cis-regulatory logic of enhancers at large scale using single-cell chromatin accessibility data to analyze regulatory elements in cell types.

feeds.title: Nature Microbiology
feeds.tldr: This digest features three recent papers from Nature Microbiology covering microbial diversity assessment, bacterial defense against horizontal gene transfer, and tuberculosis immune evasion. Each study employs advanced methodologies to reveal new insights in microbiology.
highlights: Metagenomic analysis of 92,187 samples predicts hundreds of thousands of new microbial species from unbinned contigs, with diversity following power law distributions.
YokF endonuclease in Bacillus subtilis inhibits plasmid transfer via nanotubes, and homologues are widespread in Gram-positive bacteria.
Cholesterol accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas disrupts macrophage-CD4+ T cell interactions, impairing anti-tuberculosis immunity.

items.title: Unbinned contigs expand known diversity in the global microbiome
items.tldr: A comprehensive metagenomic study quantifies discoverable bacterial and archaeal diversity, predicting approximately 705,000 bacterial and 27,000 archaeal species-level clades, largely from unbinned contigs, and shows clade size distributions follow power laws across the Tree of Life.

items.title: A family of endonucleases blocks nanotube-mediated plasmid exchange
items.tldr: The YokF endonuclease provides a donor-controlled mechanism to limit plasmid transfer to recipient bacteria via nanotubes in Bacillus subtilis, with functional homologues identified across Gram-positive bacterial species.

items.title: Lipid accumulation in tuberculosis granulomas inhibits macrophage–CD4 + T cell interactions and infection control
items.tldr: Spatial transcriptomics and microscopy reveal that infection-driven cholesterol overload in tuberculosis granulomas impairs macrophage-CD4+ T cell interactions and compromises anti-tuberculosis immunity in human and mouse models.
